frameworks.name: dopaminergic reward learning
frameworks.one_line: pupil and blink as autonomic readouts of dopamine-mediated reward signaling

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: brain learns from environment interactions predicting reward procurement

core_question: Do pupil dilation and eye-blink rate index dopaminergic reward processing for the mother's face in 12-month-old infants?
key_assumptions: pupil dilation and blink rate are autonomic markers of dopaminergic activity
mother is the primary source of biological and social rewards in infancy
caregiver responsiveness ratings reflect reward-value of the mother
manipulations: caregiver responsiveness ratings as continuous covariate
key_findings: pupils dilated more for own-mother than stranger faces
blinks were suppressed more for own-mother faces
effect magnitude scaled with caregiver responsiveness ratings
interpretations: pupil/blink readouts can probe dopaminergic social-reward learning in infancy
